title: calocephalus pilbarensis
intro: Calocephalus pilbarensis, also known as the Pilbara calocephalus, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family native to Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and flowers of Calocephalus pilbarensis have been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, offering benefits like reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or applied topically as a salve or poultice, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
